topic: Flagship computational upgrade of the banked bounded-manipulation RDD partial-ID result: replace the closed-form single-cutoff envelope by a strongly-polynomial min-cost-flow / total-unimodularity algorithm for sharp covariate-conditional manipulation bounds across many cutoff neighborhoods, with an explicit dual certificate recovering the parent interval as the one-cutoff case.

open_problems.open_problem: Can the parent single-cutoff bounded-manipulation RD trimming LP be upgraded to a strongly-polynomial min-cost-flow or total-unimodular algorithm across many cutoff neighborhoods with a dual certificate recovering the parent interval when there is one cutoff?

source_refs.excerpt: reducing endpoint evaluation to linear programs indexed by a candidate ratio lambda.
add Rosenman, Rajkumar, Gauriot, and Slonim's arXiv 1910.02170 discrete-RD manipulation bounds

source_refs.claim: Derives sharp bounds for sharp and fuzzy RD when the running variable is manipulated, centered on the one-cutoff RD setting.
source_refs.author: Gerard, Rokkanen, and Rothe

source_refs.claim: For discrete manipulated running variables, proposes partial-identification bounds via non-manipulator distributions and sequential convex programming rather than a global flow/TU certificate.
source_refs.author: Rosenman, Rajkumar, Gauriot, and Slonim

source_refs.claim: Studies RD designs with many thresholds under standard multi-cutoff identification conditions rather than manipulation-driven partial identification.
source_refs.author: Bertanha

open_problems.why_unsolved: The parent proposal establishes sharpness through a finite retention-weight and linear-fractional endpoint program, while the published manipulation-bounds literature gives sharp one-cutoff envelopes or numerical/sequential convex procedures. Multi-cutoff RD work studies pooled or target-distribution estimands under no-manipulation assumptions. The missing regime is a simultaneous sharp feasible set over many neighborhoods and covariate cells whose constraint matrix has exploitable network-flow or total-unimodular structure, plus a witness connecting the optimization certificate back to the parent closed-form interval.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identification formula equivalent to a min-cost-flow or transportation LP, a total-unimodularity/strong-polynomiality theorem, and a primal-dual certificate whose one-cutoff specialization equals the parent bounded-manipulation interval.

open_problems.open_problem: How can finite-cell bounded-manipulation RD bounds be extended to rich or continuous covariates while preserving sharpness and computable approximations?

source_refs.excerpt: continuous covariates would require an additional measurability and regularization layer
W in {1,...,K} is a finite predetermined-covariate cell.

source_refs.claim: Studies RD estimation with covariates under point identification; covariates are not used as manipulation-bound restrictions.
source_refs.author: Calonico, Cattaneo, Farrell, and Titiunik

source_refs.claim: Generalizes Lee bounds using conditional monotonicity with pretreatment covariates, showing covariates can sharply tighten partial-ID bounds.
source_refs.author: Semenova

source_refs.claim: Formulates partial identification with noisy covariates as a robust optimization problem, illustrating algorithmic treatment of covariate uncertainty in causal bounds.
source_refs.author: Guo, Yin, Wang, and Jordan
source_refs.venue: Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, CLeaR

open_problems.why_unsolved: The parent deliberately restricts covariates to finitely many predetermined cells; the scope flag identifies the missing measurability and regularization layer. Covariate-adjusted RD theory improves point-identified estimation, and covariate-assisted partial-ID work such as generalized Lee bounds addresses selection settings rather than manipulated running variables. In manipulated RD, continuous covariates determine both manipulation caps and outcome envelopes, so arbitrary coarsening can change the sharp feasible set.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set theorem for continuous-covariate manipulation caps, paired with a conservative or sharp approximation result for measurable retention kernels or convergent finite coarsenings.

open_problems.open_problem: What are sharp RD manipulation bounds when manipulation can be two-sided or imprecise, so density jumps may be masked and a one-sided McCrary envelope no longer identifies the contamination direction?

source_refs.excerpt: two-sided manipulation can mask density jumps and would need a different sensitivity parameter.

source_refs.claim: Introduces the density test for manipulation in RD, formalizing discontinuities in the running-variable density as evidence of sorting.
source_refs.author: McCrary

source_refs.claim: Improves manipulation testing through boundary-adaptive local-polynomial density estimation and principled inference.
source_refs.author: Cattaneo, Jansson, and Ma
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Argues that imprecise manipulation can destroy point identification and that not all harmful manipulations are detectable by diagnostic tests.
source_refs.author: Ishihara and Sawada

open_problems.why_unsolved: The parent assumes one-sided upward manipulation and builds the feasible set through right-side removal or retention. Density-test papers estimate discontinuities in the running-variable density, while manipulation-robust identification requires restrictions on how manipulated and non-manipulated units differ in potential outcomes. Two-sided or imprecise manipulation changes the observational equivalence class because inflows and outflows can offset in the density diagnostic, leaving the parent McCrary-envelope parameter insufficient.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp partial-identification interval, or a counterexample with regime statement, for a two-sided/imprecise manipulation sensitivity model with explicit parameters for masked inflow and outflow.

open_problems.open_problem: Can the fuzzy-RD exact-share face under bounded manipulation be characterized by a sharp computational dual when outcome and treatment retention are coupled rather than reducible to ordinary Lee outcome-tail trimming?

source_refs.excerpt: the full Wald endpoint program because outcome and treatment retention are coupled.
replace it with a fuzzy exact-share reduction that matches the GRR fuzzy optimization

open_problems.why_unsolved: The parent narrows the exact-share Lee reduction to the sharp-RD face and explicitly leaves fuzzy RD inside the full Wald endpoint program. Reviewers flagged that fuzzy exact-share bounds involve treatment-status-specific manipulator shares and coupled numerator/denominator retention, so ordinary outcome-tail trimming is not a sharpness certificate.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sufficient-condition or duality theorem for the fuzzy exact-share endpoint program, identifying the coupled retention scores and a sharp primal-dual certificate.

open_problems.open_problem: What general conditions determine when external cell caps strictly delete the cell-cap-free GRR envelope face, beyond the parent local finite-support witness class?

source_refs.excerpt: add an explicit global condition that no feasible retained mass above m^c can exceed U(m^c)
reframed Conjecture ref{conj:cap-separation} as a positive open-set separation

open_problems.why_unsolved: The parent proposal and reviewer trail focus on an auditable two-cell or local open-set separation. The reviewer-requested global condition points to the remaining gap: a reusable characterization of when cap-induced retained-mass constraints remove all worse cell-cap-free envelope faces, instead of only verifying a constructed witness.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient active-set or dual-certificate theorem for cap-envelope separation over a general finite covariate support.

open_problems.open_problem: How should uniform inference be conducted for manipulation-bound endpoints estimated as intersections or optima over many cutoff neighborhoods and covariate cells?

source_refs.claim: Constructs valid confidence intervals for manipulated-RD bounds, but not for a many-cutoff covariate-indexed intersection endpoint.
source_refs.author: Gerard, Rokkanen, and Rothe

source_refs.claim: Develops inference for bounds characterized by intersections of conditional inequalities, relevant to endpoint estimation over rich index sets.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen

open_problems.why_unsolved: Manipulation bounds depend on estimated density or mass deficits, outcome envelopes, and possibly data-selected neighborhoods. Intersection-bounds theory handles rich index sets abstractly, but it does not incorporate the coupled RD manipulation geometry; density-test inference likewise does not propagate into sharp partial-ID optimization endpoints.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform confidence region or endpoint confidence band for the sharp bound support function over the cutoff-neighborhood and covariate-cell index class.

literature_map:
Gerard, Rokkanen, and Rothe (2020, Quantitative Economics) is the core manipulated-RD partial-ID anchor: manipulation destroys point identification, but sharp bounds and confidence intervals are possible when manipulation extent is inferred from data.
McCrary (2008, Journal of Econometrics) and Cattaneo, Jansson, and Ma (2020, JASA) anchor density-discontinuity diagnostics; these tests do not themselves deliver downstream causal bounds.
Ishihara and Sawada (2024, arXiv) sharpen the detectability gap: imprecise manipulation may be harmful and not all harmful manipulation is detectable.
Rosenman, Rajkumar, Gauriot, and Slonim (2019, arXiv) propose optimized partial-ID bounds for discrete manipulated RD using sequential convex programming, not a global flow/TU certificate.
Cattaneo, Keele, Titiunik, and Vazquez-Bare (2021, JASA) develop multi-cutoff RD extrapolation under validity assumptions.
Bertanha (2020, Journal of Econometrics) studies RD with many thresholds and target distributions, highlighting identification restrictions for multi-cutoff settings.
Cattaneo, Titiunik, and Vazquez-Bare (2020, Stata Journal) provide software and inference for multiple cutoffs/scores, but not manipulation-bounds optimization.
Calonico, Cattaneo, Farrell, and Titiunik (2019, RESTAT) clarify covariate adjustment in point-identified RD; this differs from covariates as partial-ID restrictions.
Semenova (2025, Journal of Econometrics) shows conditional covariates can tighten Lee-type partial-ID bounds, suggesting an analogy for manipulated RD.
Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen (2013, Econometrica) provide intersection-bound inference tools relevant to estimated envelopes over covariate/cutoff index sets.
Optimization-oriented partial-ID work such as Guo, Yin, Wang, and Jordan (2022, CLeaR) shows a live route from sharp bounds to computational certificates.
The main open substrate is a many-cutoff, covariate-conditional, manipulation-robust RD bound with a strongly polynomial network-flow/TU algorithm and interpretable dual certificate.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the parent banked entry first: CausalSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/candidates/pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1/README.md, proposal.tex, pipeline.jsonl, and reviewer JSONs angle0_v1 through angle0_v4 plus Stage 0.5 review files.
The parent README records an accepted bounded-manipulation RD partial-ID result but leaves reusable-artifact and gap-reason fields as TODOs.
The proposal section 11 Honest scope flags yielded finite-cell, one-sided-manipulation, and fuzzy-Wald coupling limitations.
The proposal proof route yielded the computational substrate: endpoint evaluation is described as linear/fractional programming indexed by a candidate ratio, not as a flow/TU algorithm.
Reviewer angle0_v1 and angle0_v3 supplied strengthening paths around fuzzy exact-share GRR comparisons, finite worked witnesses, and adjacent computational RD-manipulation prior art.
Reviewer angle0_v4 supplied a strengthening path asking for a global condition behind the cap-envelope separation witness.
The current qid directory pid_rdd_flow_manipulation contains only run state/pipeline initialization and no proposal artifacts yet.
Study-pipeline gaps were mostly LATE, Manski bounds, and interval-regression substrate; none was used as a direct local source for this RDD computation track.
